summary: Frontend E2E validation via Playwright. OpeningTour (auto-open, 4 steps, mute/skip/next/prev/progress, Começar closes, Navbar TOUR re-opens) PASS. MixerSwirl card+canvas+loading state ('Gerando vídeo…' disabled after 15s) PASS. Productions3D card+canvas+shape switching (bandeja/colar) PASS, but generate flow has issues. CRITICAL: 13 runtime page errors on /studio related to React Three Fiber: 'R3F: Cannot set x-line-number' (3x) and 'Cannot convert undefined or null to object' (10x). React error overlay is visible to user on /studio page. Generate button re-enables in ~1s with NO success toast — Nano Banana flow appears to fail silently or component crashes before fetch.

ui_bugs.issue: Uncaught runtime errors flooding /studio page: 'R3F: Cannot set "x-line-number". Ensure it is an object before setting "line-number".' followed by multiple 'Cannot convert undefined or null to object' (removeChild/removeChildFromContainer in R3F reconciler). React error overlay (CRA) appears in dev mode. Errors repeat on every Studio mount/unmount.

After clicking [data-testid="prod3d-generate-btn"], button re-enables after ~1s and NO success toast appears. Expected: Nano Banana takes ~10-25s and toast shows on completion. Likely the R3F crash interrupts state OR the request errors out silently (no error toast shown either).

integration_issues.issue: R3F reconciler errors during mount cause Cannot convert undefined or null to object on removeChild. Visible to dev/preview users via error overlay. Likely caused by Babel JSX __source plugin injecting fileName/lineNumber that R3F tries to apply to Three.js objects (known R3F issue with non-Three children, JSX whitespace, or babel-plugin-transform-react-jsx-source). Also could be caused by drei <Environment preset='studio'> failing to load HDRI without proper Suspense fallback, or by passing data-testid on <Canvas>.

action_items: Fix R3F runtime errors on /studio (Productions3D.jsx). Investigate: (1) remove data-testid from <Canvas> and place it on a wrapping div, (2) ensure no stray whitespace/text/comments inside <Canvas>, (3) verify @react-three/drei <Environment preset='studio'> works in this environment - try removing it as a quick test, (4) check @react-three/fiber version vs React 18 compatibility, (5) check for babel-plugin-transform-react-jsx-source interference.
Add error handling + toast to Productions3D handleGenerate so failures surface to user instead of silently re-enabling the button.
Verify Nano Banana endpoint is actually being hit during generate (currently no network confirmation since button releases in 1s).
critical_code_review_comments: Productions3D.jsx line 229: data-testid on <Canvas> — R3F forwards extra props to the wrapper but this combined with __source can collide. Move data-testid to outer div for safety.
Productions3D.jsx generate flow: no try/catch toast surfacing when Nano Banana fails — silent failure UX.
Productions3D.jsx <Environment preset='studio' /> requires external HDR; can throw if network fails. Wrap in error boundary or use simpler lighting.
seed_data_creation: None. Cleared localStorage('lindart.tour.v1.seen') to force tour auto-open.
context_for_next_testing_agent: OpeningTour & MixerSwirl flows fully validated PASS. Focus next iteration on /studio R3F errors and Productions3D generate→toast flow. Use localStorage.removeItem('lindart.tour.v1.seen') before testing tour. Use REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL from frontend/.env.
rca_of_the_issue: ROOT CAUSE (Productions3D R3F errors): React Three Fiber reconciler is attempting to apply props/__source metadata to a non-Three.js child within <Canvas>. The error 'Cannot set x-line-number' is R3F internal handling of `line-number` (from React's __source debug info) on an object that isn't a Three.js instance. Cascading 'Cannot convert undefined or null to object' errors on removeChild indicate the Three scene graph is in an inconsistent state on unmount. REPRODUCTION: Navigate to /studio in dev mode — runtime error overlay appears. MITIGATION OPTIONS: (a) Move data-testid off <Canvas> to wrapper div; (b) Remove <Environment preset='studio'> temporarily to isolate; (c) Pin @react-three/fiber and @react-three/drei to known-good versions; (d) Add an ErrorBoundary around <Canvas>.
